headline: Barclays Downgrades HCA Healthcare (HCA) – Here’s Why
summary: HCA Healthcare, Inc. (NYSE:HCA) is one of the top cheap blue chip stocks to buy according to Wall Street analysts. Barclays downgraded HCA Healthcare, Inc. (NYSE:HCA) to Equal Weight from Overweight on July 8, bringing the price target on the stock down to $427 from $496. The firm told investors that following a strong three-year […]

headline: HCA Healthcare (HCA) Stock Sees Modest Fair Value Cut As Policy Risks Temper Growth Views
summary: HCA Healthcare is in focus after its modeled fair value was trimmed from US$503.57 to US$490.29, reflecting updated assumptions in the latest analyst work. This shift lines up with recent research that leans more cautious on long term growth and policy risk, even as some analysts still point to support from core operations and certain volume trends. As you read on, you will see how these pricing updates fit into the broader Street narrative on HCA Healthcare and what to watch to keep up with...

headline: Why Elevance Earnings Gave Health Insurance Stocks A Cold; UNH Up Next
summary: Elevance Health topped Q2 earnings estimates Wednesday, despite falling medical membership, but profit was boosted by one-time "below-the-line" factors and the full-year outlook was underwhelming.  ELV stock tumbled, making it among the S&P 500's early laggards.  Molina Healthcare, another S&P 500 managed care stock, joined Elevance among the biggest laggards in pre-market trading.

headline: HCA Healthcare (HCA) Stock Still Trades At A Discount Despite A 52% Return
summary: HCA Healthcare stock has fallen sharply in the short term, yet over five years it is still in positive territory and currently screens as undervalued on several valuation checks. This sets up a clear tension between recent sentiment and longer term returns. HCA Healthcare has returned 52.3% over the past five years, so long term holders remain ahead even after recent share price pressure. Expectations around HCA Healthcare’s use of scale and AI tools to support care quality can help the...

headline: Why HCA Healthcare (HCA) Is Down 14.1% After Cutting 2026 Guidance on Uninsured Patient Shift
summary: In July 2026, HCA Healthcare, Inc. reported preliminary second-quarter 2026 results showing revenue of about US$20.23 billion and net income of about US$1.70 billion, while also lowering its full-year 2026 earnings guidance due to a payer mix shift toward more uninsured patients following changes in health insurance exchange coverage. An important nuance is that HCA expects an unfavorable impact on income before taxes from this payer mix shift, only partly offset by incremental benefits from...
